---
title: eda.plots.plot_ip_histograms
---



```python
eda.plots.plot_ip_histograms(
    df,
    bins=10,
    num_cols=2,
    figwidth=10,
    thrs_unique=5,
    add_points=None,
    add_points_col=['red'],
)
```

Generate infill-point histograms (ip-histograms) for each numerical column in the DataFrame within a single figure.
The title of each histogram shows the total, unique values count, outliers, and standard deviation.
If there are fewer unique values than the threshold thrs_unique, the ip-histogram is colored differently.
Additional points can be added and highlighted in red.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name           | Type                                           | Description                                                            | Default    |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| df             | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | DataFrame containing the data to plot.                                 | _required_ |
| bins           | [int](`int`)                                   | Number of bins for the histograms. Defaults to 10.                     | `10`       |
| num_cols       | [int](`int`)                                   | Number of columns in the subplot grid. Defaults to 2.                  | `2`        |
| figwidth       | [int](`int`)                                   | Width of the entire figure. Defaults to 10.                            | `10`       |
| thrs_unique    | [int](`int`)                                   | Threshold for unique values to change histogram color. Defaults to 5.  | `5`        |
| add_points     | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | DataFrame containing additional points to highlight. Defaults to None. | `None`     |
| add_points_col | [list](`list`)                                 | List of colors for the additional points. Defaults to ["red"].         | `['red']`  |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description   |
|--------|--------|---------------|
|        | None   | None          |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}


::: {#717cf665 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
from spotoptim.eda.plots import plot_ip_histograms
data = {'A': [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 5, 100], 'B': [10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
plot_ip_histograms(df, bins=5, num_cols=1, thrs_unique=3)
# Example with multiple added points and colors
add_points = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1.5, 3.5], 'B': [10, 10]})
plot_ip_histograms(df, add_points=add_points, add_points_col=["red", "blue"])
```
